NCT02675114.name: PARTNER 3
NCT02675114.group: Low-risk symptomatic severe AS (STS <4%), SAPIEN 3 TAVR vs SAVR
NCT02675114.snippet: Source: Mack MJ et al. NEJM 2019;380:1695-1705 (PARTNER 3).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Mack MJ et al. NEJM 2019;380:1695-1705, CONSORT
evidence.text: 1,000 low-risk (STS <4%) patients with severe symptomatic aortic stenosis were randomized 1:1 to TAVR with SAPIEN 3 transcatheter heart valve (n=503) or surgical aortic valve replacement (n=497). Analysis cohort: 496 TAVR / 454 SAVR who had valve implantation.

evidence.label: Primary — 1-year ACM/Stroke/Rehospitalization
evidence.source: Mack MJ et al. NEJM 2019, Figure 1
evidence.text: Primary composite at 1 year: 42/496 (8.5%) TAVR vs 68/454 (15.1%) SAVR. HR 0.54 (95% CI 0.37-0.79; P<0.001 for superiority). Kaplan-Meier curves separate at 30 days and remain divergent through 12 months.

evidence.label: 5-year Durability
evidence.text: At 5 years the composite primary narrowed: 22.8% TAVR vs 27.2% SAVR; HR 0.79 (0.61-1.04). Early TAVR benefit partially attenuated but not reversed; no difference in mortality alone.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~1. Severe, calcific aortic stenosis~2. New York Heart Association Functional Class ≥ 2 OR exercise tolerance test that demonstrates a limited exercise capacity, abnormal BP response, or arrhythmia OR asymptomatic with Left Ventricular Ejection Fraction (LVEF) <50%~3. Heart team agrees the patient has a risk of operative mortality and has an Society of Thoracic Surgeons (STS) score < 4~4. The study patient has been informed of the nature of the study, agrees to its provisio...

NCT02701283.name: Evolut Low Risk
NCT02701283.group: Low-risk symptomatic severe AS (STS <3%), Evolut self-expanding TAVR vs SAVR
NCT02701283.snippet: Source: Popma JJ et al. NEJM 2019;380:1706-1715 (Evolut Low Risk).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.text: 1,468 patients with severe symptomatic aortic stenosis at low surgical risk (STS <3%) were randomized to self-expanding Evolut R, Evolut PRO, or Evolut PRO+ TAVR (n=734) vs SAVR (n=734). Attempted-procedure cohort: 725 TAVR / 678 SAVR.

evidence.label: Primary — 24-month ACM or Disabling Stroke (Bayesian)
evidence.text: Estimated 24-month KM rate: 5.3% TAVR vs 6.7% SAVR. Posterior probability of non-inferiority >0.99. Absolute event counts (attempted): 38 TAVR vs 49 SAVR; HR 0.72 (0.47-1.10) at 2 years.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~1. Severe aortic stenosis, defined as follows:~   1. For symptomatic patients:~      Aortic valve area ≤1.0 cm2 (or aortic valve area index of ≤0.6 cm2/m2), OR mean gradient ≥40 mmHg, OR Maximal aortic valve velocity ≥4.0 m/sec by transthoracic echocardiography at rest~  2. For asymptomatic patients:~      * Very severe aortic stenosis with an aortic valve area of ≤1.0 cm2 (or aortic valve area index of ≤0.6 cm2/m2), AND maximal aortic velocity ≥5.0 m/sec , or mean gradient ...

NCT01057173.name: NOTION
NCT01057173.group: Elderly (>=70y) low-risk AS, CoreValve TAVR vs SAVR
NCT01057173.snippet: Source: Thyregod HG et al. JACC 2015;65:2184-2194 (NOTION); long-term Thyregod et al. JAMA 2022.

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.text: 280 patients aged >=70 years with severe aortic stenosis at lower surgical risk (mean STS ~2.9%) randomized to self-expanding CoreValve TAVR (n=145) vs SAVR (n=135) in Nordic countries.

evidence.label: Primary — 1-year ACM/Stroke/MI
evidence.text: 1-year composite: 19/145 (13.1%) TAVR vs 22/135 (16.3%) SAVR. HR 0.79 (95% CI 0.44-1.41; P=0.43). Non-significant; no mortality difference.

evidence.label: 10-year Long-Term (JAMA 2022)
evidence.text: 10-year composite ACM/stroke/MI: 65.5% TAVR vs 65.5% SAVR; HR 1.00. Durable equivalence; no late TAVR disadvantage emerged in this lower-risk elderly cohort.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 70 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Patients must have severe degenerative aortic valve stenosis (echocardiographic criteria: AV effective orifice area (EOA) of < 1 cm2, mean AV gradient of > 40 mmHg, or AV peak systolic velocity of > 4.0 m/s)~* Patients must be symptomatic from the aortic valve stenosis (dyspnoea in NYHA-class II or greater, angina pectoris, or syncope), or asymptomatic but with echocardiographic evidence of left ventricular hypertrophy, decreased left ventricular ejection fraction, or a...

title: RapidMeta Cardiology | TAVR in Low-Risk Aortic Stenosis v1.0
